item.title: Cabo Verde's World Cup party just getting started after another thrilling draw against Uruguay
item.description: Cabo Verde scored its first ever World Cup goal, then came from behind to secure just its second ever point by holding Uruguay to a thrilling 2-2 draw that puts the Tubarões Azuis in with a great chance of qualifying for the knock out stages.

item.title: Second case of deadly H5 bird flu confirmed in southern WA
item.description: Authorities say a second bird found sick on WA's south coast had the deadly H5 strain of avian flu, as one of the nation's biggest chicken producers announces it is locking down its WA operations.

item.title: Agriculture Minister confirms another case of H5 bird flu in Western Australia
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Labor MP calls for Lord's Prayer to be scrapped in WA parliament
item.description: A Labor MP says he wants the Lord’s Prayer scrapped from WA parliament, despite the reading of the Christian prayer being a long-held tradition before sessions.

item.title: Four of our best nourishing stew recipes for winter weeknight dinners
item.description: From a hearty beef and veggie blend to chicken and vegetarian options, these are some of our favourite stew recipes for cosy nights in.

item.title: Video shows the moment Iran walks out of the room, leaving JD Vance to promote peace talks
item.description: In a room in a glamorous Swiss resort, the United States delegation, Pakistani and Qatari officials were awaiting the arrival of the Iranian representatives. But as Tehran's men enter the room, they appear to notice the press.

item.title: Three killed in school shooting in Tacloban, central Philippines
item.description: At least three students have been killed and seven others injured in a rare school shooting incident in the central Philippines, according to police.

item.title: Newcastle Knights player facing judiciary after alleged homophobic slur in NSW Cup match
item.description: Asu Kepaoa will face the NSW Rugby League judiciary after being sent off in a NSW Cup game for allegedly using a homophobic slur.

item.title: Rugby Australia's new director of women's high-performance Tim Walsh sets eyes on 2029 World Cup
item.description: Only three years out from hosting the Women's Rugby World Cup, Australia makes a key appointment it hopes will help deliver success on the international stage.

item.title: Runaway Tasmanian devil 'Mary' discharged from hospital
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Queensland budget to include $545 million to boost water security, restore dams
item.description: The treasurer has also promised no cuts to public service, but won't commit to returning to surplus within the next four years.

item.title: Two men arrested, cars torched after violent disturbance in Yuendumu, Central Australia
item.description: Two men have been arrested and denied bail after a violent disturbance in Yuendumu, in which multiple vehicles were allegedly set alight by armed offenders.

item.title: Keir Starmer announces resignation as UK Prime Minister
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Monday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: AFL legend Tony Modra's family 'extremely grateful' for support after crash
item.description: The family of AFL icon Tony Modra has released a statement thanking the community for the "overwhelming show of love" they have received since he was seriously injured in a crash last week.

item.title: Deputy coroner releases findings into death of teen who took his own life
item.description: A 17-year-old school captain, who was dux of year 12 and a talented athlete, appeared to have everything ahead of him. Then he took his own life.

item.title: First H5N1 bird flu case detected in Australia
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: From Toy Story's return to pitch-perfect Australian horror, here's what's in cinemas this month
item.description: Plus Toy Story 5 trots out the same old games, Australian horror scores another winner and Colony uses zombies to ring the warning bell about AI.

item.title: JD Vance says Iran has agreed to nuclear inspections as talks progress
item.description: The US vice president says that progress has been made in talks with Iran, with Tehran agreeing to allow nuclear inspectors and a mechanism to keep the Strait of Hormuz open.

item.title: Haaland, Mbappe and Messi make it another magic World Cup day
item.description: Lionel Messi breaks a World Cup scoring record, Kylian Mbappe and France put the foot down against Iraq after big weather delay, Erling Haaland strikes again for Norway and Jordan is eliminated.

item.title: The Rosetta Stone was taken from Egypt. This artist took it back
item.description: The Rosetta Stone changed the way we understood Ancient Egypt. It sits in the British Museum but this artist wanted to return it home. To pull it off he needed some legal advice, a digital scanner and the courage to take on the way we look at history.

item.title: Quiz: Do you know a lychee from a rambutan? Name these fruits
item.description: Can you tell these tropical fruits apart? It's not as easy as you think.

item.title: The fake ABC News articles trying to sell you a scam
item.description: A $350 million scam is targeting Australians using AI-generated images of ABC journalists and politicians.

item.title: Private companies want to geoengineer the sky. The rain billions of people depend on could be disrupted
item.description: Private companies want to reflect the Sun to cool the planet. But scientists fear the geoengineering technology could disrupt monsoon rains, on which a billion people depend.

item.title: ABC More or Less quiz: British PMs edition
item.description: Some British PMs have held office across decades. Others have struggled to outlast a lettuce. It's time to test your knowledge.

item.title: Will SpaceX bring stocks thudding back to earth?
item.description: As we plough on through the fourth consecutive year of global stock records and huge annual advances, it's not surprising that many pundits have begun to question just how much longer this boom can continue.

item.title: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson backs Ben Roberts-Smith invite to War Memorial opening
item.description: Federal parliament will adjourn early today so politicians can attend the official opening of a hall as part of the controversial $550 million extension of the War Memorial.

item.title: Plan to ease congestion on Hobart's Southern Outlet hit by another cost blowout
item.description: The cost of widening Hobart's Southern Outlet has blown out by another $32 million, with completion of the northern section of the project now estimated at $95 million.

item.title: Australian Labor learnt from leadership chaos. Britain could be about to
item.description: After the era of Rudd-Gillard-Rudd, the ALP said: "never again." Sir Keir Starmer's resignation on Monday means we're about to find out if Britain's Labour MPs were watching.

item.title: World Cup: Lionel Messi's record-breaking goals provide spiritual experience in Dallas
item.description: Lionel Messi now stands alone with a record 18 World Cup goals. And for those who witnessed it, it was a transcendent experience.

item.title: MV Hondius passengers cleared of hantavirus after 42 days of quarantine in WA
item.description: Six passengers from Australia and NZ linked to the deadly MV Hondius hantavirus outbreak are on their way home after completing 42 days of quarantine in Perth and being cleared to leave.

item.title: This 'King of the North' could become the next UK PM. But who is Andy Burnham?
item.description: A popular mayor but a virtual unknown on the global stage, Andy Burnham is poised to become Sir Keir Starmer's successor. Here's what that could look like.

item.title: Andy Burnham discusses COVID-19 pandemic in 2020
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Farmers face nervous wait as bird flu investigations and testing widen in WA
item.description: Producers across an enormous stretch of southern WA are facing a tense few days, as authorities investigate more than 50 dead birds found along the state's southern coast.

item.title: Child protection advocates slam decision to scrap SA social worker registration scheme
item.description: Child protection advocates say the South Australian government has let down the state's most vulnerable people by axing a mandatory registration scheme for social workers, but those in the DV sector say the move could help alleviate workforce shortages.

item.title: NSW budget to be delivered today. Here's what we know so far
item.description: NSW Treasurer Daniel Mookhey has tempered expectations of a return to surplus ahead of Tuesday's budget. Here's what has been promised so far.

item.title: Veterinary industry pushes government for student placement support as regional shortages persist
item.description: Veterinarians are calling for more government support to attract and retain professionals in rural Australia where it often takes more than a year to fill jobs.

item.title: What's been achieved by the US–Iran talks in Switzerland so far
item.description: The US claims "great progress" has been made with Iran during the first substantial talks to take place under the new deal to pause the war, but there are already conflicting messages from Iran. Here's what we know about the results of the negotiations so far.

item.title: Sustainable Timber Tasmania gives new answer to parliament over logs sent to Victoria
item.description: Sustainable Timber Tasmania has corrected the record after earlier stating that all logs from public native forests are processed in Tasmania.

item.title: Hanson says Ben Roberts-Smith absence from Anzac Hall opening 'disappointing' — as it happened
item.description: Victoria Cross recipient and alleged war criminal Ben Roberts-Smith will no longer attend an event at the Australian War Memorial tonight. Take a look back at how the day unfolded.

item.title: Tennant Creek unrest turned fatal sparks anger over paused mediation program
item.description: Mediation projects were a key part of the Barkly Regional Deal signed in 2019, but many locals are asking why the process seemed to have halted prior to the stabbing death of a young man amid unrest in Tennant Creek.

item.title: Multicultural communities still struggle with emergency messaging in times of crisis
item.description: Multicultural communities may not automatically understand the intent behind Australia's emergency messaging, but there are ways to fix this.

item.title: Ex-Wimbledon champion Markéta Vondroušová suspended for refusing doping test
item.description: Markéta Vondroušová, who lifted the Wimbledon trophy in 2023, is given a "significant ban" by the International Tennis Integrity Agency.

item.title: Passengers of hantavirus-hit cruise ship leave quarantine facility in WA
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Community concern over doubling of water release from Menindee Lakes
item.description: Residents living along the Darling River are worried the decision to double water releases from the Menindee Lakes will remove the system's drought reserve. But authorities say it will help rebuild populations of Murray cod downstream.

item.title: Blues dealt fresh blow with Latrell Mitchell ruled out of State of Origin III
item.description: After a demoralising defeat to the Maroons, the Blues learn Latrell Mitchell will be unavailable for selection in the series-deciding State of Origin III.

item.title: WiseTech executive chair Richard White denies human trafficking reports — as it happened
item.description: WiseTech shares finished the day lower by 3.3 per cent after Monday's 18 per cent dive, as executive chair Richard White "emphatically and unequivocally" has denied human trafficking reports. The ASX 200 closed 0.3 per cent lower.

item.title: Bird flu has arrived in Australia
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Children in hot car among at least 18 dead in France as Europe bakes
item.description: At least 18 people died in France, including two children left in a hot car, as a heatwave hung over Europe, smashing temperature records in several cities.

item.title: Farmers say absentee landholders hindering battle to control feral pests
item.description: Farmers says some absentee landholders, who do not live on the land full-time, are not pulling their weight when it comes to feral pest management.

item.title: Pauline Hanson says she supports paid parental leave after questioning pay for women 'not at work'
item.description: Pauline Hanson has clarified she would not seek to cut government-funded paid parental leave if One Nation were in power after opening the door to a wind-back at her National Press Club address last week.

item.title: Thirteen killed and dozens injured in blast in Qatar's Ras Laffan gas processing facility
item.description: The Sunday night blast in Qatar's Ras Laffan gas processing facility is one of the deadliest events recorded in gas industry.

item.title: KPMG chair Martin Sheppard resigns amid whistleblower fallout
item.description: KPMG chair Martin Sheppard and two audit partners are leaving the accounting and consulting giant in the wake of a scandal over misuse of confidential client information and the alleged mistreatment of a whistleblower.

item.title: Victoria's Doreen Egg farm admits to misleading free-range labelling
item.description: Doreen Egg admits it sold eggs labelled as "free range" despite its hens not having access to an outdoor range, as required under Free Range Egg Labelling Information Standard.

item.title: Health warning issued after powerful sedative etomidate was found in illegal vapes in Victoria
item.description: Victorian health authorities say vape products containing the powerful sedative etomidate have been found being sold illegally.

item.title: Greens back CGT, negative gearing changes in return for extended NDIS inquiry, end to super loophole
item.description: The Greens pledge their support in exchange for an end to an exemption that allows self-managed super funds to borrow money to invest in residential property, and a delay on the overhaul of the NDIS.

item.title: How one letter gifted Cheryl her Indigenous identity — and two siblings
item.description: Cheryl van Wageningen was shocked to receive a letter revealing that not only was she Indigenous, but she had a brother and a sister.

item.title: Music mogul Clive Davis dies at 94 leaving behind powerful imprint on industry
item.description: Clive Davis, the influential music industry mogul who fostered the careers of superstars such as Whitney Houston, Bruce Springsteen and Alicia Keys, has died. He was 94.

item.title: The Doyles $20k award-winning artwork revealed as 'blatant copy'
item.description: The Doyles award committee is investigating why it took nearly 12 months to discover a winning landscape painting was an imitation of another artist's work.

item.title: Newly discovered ballista spider
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Lila McGuire celebrates success of First Nations talent in Logie nominations
item.description: With strong representation of Indigenous talent in this year's Logie Award nominations, actor Lila McGuire says it's a "very important time" and "a gift for young mob seeing so many of us excelling".

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Wild weather, Messi magic and Merlin meets the president
item.description: Some wild weather brings the World Cup to a standstill, but not before Lionel Messi lights it up to rewrite the record books. Here are today's quick hits.

item.title: Indian medical exam leaks scandal sparks a resit, protests and tragedy
item.description: More than 2 million students across India have resat the country's highly competitive medical entrance exam after last month's test was cancelled amid allegations that questions had been leaked.

item.title: The winners and losers in the 2026 NSW budget
item.description: With billions for health but limited funds for the state's regions, here are the 2026 NSW budget's winners and losers.

item.title: Police locate body during search for missing Horsham man Neville Nicholson
item.description: Police say the body of a male was located during the search for Victorian man Neville Nicholson who has been missing since last Wednesday.

item.title: New spider discovered in Queensland uses 'ballista' web to trap prey
item.description: A bizarre species of spider from the rainforests of Far North Queensland springs a nasty surprise.

item.title: Queensland budget 2026: Winners and losers
item.description: The Queensland treasurer has handed down the state budget. Take a look at what it includes and who benefits.

item.title: Former church youth leader sentenced for sexually assaulting teenager
item.description: A man who assaulted a girl in his car has dodged a lengthy jail term, with a judge saying there were "exceptional circumstances" that warranted a discounted sentence.

item.title: Newly discovered ballista spider
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: ACEREZ investigation finds failures during construction led to destruction of Aboriginal rock shelter
item.description: ACEREZ introduces strict cultural heritage protocols after a rock shelter was irreparably damaged.

item.title: Dead birds to be tested for H5N1 bird flu after washing up on remote SA coast
item.description: Three dead seabirds have been collected and are being tested for the H5N1 bird flu virus in South Australia, as well as another two wild birds in Western Australia, following Australia's first confirmed cases of the deadly variant this week.

item.title: Disability advocates welcome extension after 'ridiculous and disrespectful' NDIS inquiry
item.description: Disability advocates welcome an extension to a Senate inquiry probing a bill paving the way for the biggest-ever cuts to the NDIS.

item.title: Sunshine Coast students push back as Australia positions itself as a global AI leader
item.description: As Australia tracks toward becoming a global leader in artificial intelligence infrastructure, a group of high school students is demanding stronger regulation.

item.title: Virgin Australia allows pets in plane cabin in expansion of Melbourne-Queensland pilot
item.description: An Australian airline is allowing small cats and dogs on a range of flights, with other passengers appearing unconcerned about sharing the plane with furry creatures.

item.title: Tasmanian egg producers brace for deadly strain of bird flu, potential mass euthanasia
item.description: Tasmanian poultry producers are ramping up biosecurity measures, as they prepare for the arrival of a deadly strain of bird flu. The state government has stressed that it's well prepared, but says euthanasia is "definitely possible" if there's an outbreak in commercial poultry.

item.title: SA government moves to regulate AI data centres as water, energy concerns mount
item.description: The South Australian government is pushing for the state to become home to billions of dollars' worth of artificial intelligence data centres, but says it will seek to regulate their water and energy use.

item.title: Wagga Wagga man fined for selling horse meat to greyhound trainers
item.description: A Wagga Wagga man will be more than $40,000 out of pocket after selling tonnes of horse meat to dog trainers in NSW and Victoria.

item.title: World Cup: Alessandro Circati was born in Italy. His dad is happy he chose the Socceroos
item.description: Alessandro Circati's decision to play for the Socceroos raised eyebrows within his family in Italy, but his father says they now realise that, deep down, he's not Italian.

item.title: 'Shaman' and partner withheld magic mushrooms use before Adelaide fashion icon's death, inquest told
item.description: A coronial inquest is investigating the death of Adelaide fashion icon Carol Foord, who used magic mushrooms as an alternative treatment prior to her death in June 2024.

item.title: Angus Taylor refuses to commit to a multicultural Australia under repeated questioning
item.description: The opposition leader, pressed multiple times on whether his party still supported multiculturalism or endorsed One Nation leader Pauline Hanson's vision of a "monoculture", refused to commit to either.

item.title: Senior Albanese minister clashes with Pocock over top-secret AI copyright claim
item.description: Federal politician David Pocock has clashed with a senior Albanese government minister over a whistleblower claim that federal cabinet is considering plans to let AI companies train on Australian copyright material.

item.title: Kids Helpline crisis interventions again on the rise, sparking concern about return to COVID-era levels
item.description: Kids Helpline says a growing number of Australian young people are requiring immediate emergency help, with a concerning trend around crisis interventions.

item.title: Daryl Braithwaite steps back from live performances, saying the joy has gone
item.description: Australian vocalist, former Sherbet frontman and ARIA Hall of Fame inductee Daryl Braithwaite says he is stepping back from singing live.

item.title: Tuesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Colombia scores late winner against Congo, England frustrated by resolute Ghana
item.description: The World Cup looks set for a second 0-0 draw before a late strike by Daniel Muñoz sees Colombia to a 1-0 win over DR Congo to sit atop Group K despite Portugal's 5-0 victory over Uzbekistan.

item.title: Gardening Australia star proud to be out after being 'de-gayed for TV for 26 years'
item.description: Gardening Australia star Jerry Coleby-Williams hid his identity on TV for 26 years, but has never been ashamed of being a gay man.

item.title: Travel quiz, June 24: Join us for a mid-week trip around the world
item.description: Which town would you be in if you were sunbathing at Cable Beach? Where does Maldon sea salt come from?

item.title: Cosy ways to keep warm this winter without turning the heater on
item.description: Heating your whole house — or even a room — can be expensive in the depths of winter. Keeping the humans inside it warm is often cheaper and more efficient.

item.title: Port Moresby's water supply network showing 'signs of imminent failure'
item.description: A decades-old pipeline supplying water to nearly 1 million people in Papua New Guinea's capital is at risk of collapsing without urgent repairs, threatening to cut off more than half of the city's water supply.

item.title: Tax experts and self-managed super funds criticise Labor–Greens deal as 'policy on the run'
item.description: A ban on self-managed super funds borrowing money to buy housing has blindsided the sector, which says Labor should instead target "unscrupulous" operators spruiking property as an investment.

item.title: National Suicide Prevention Outcomes Map to collect data about what services work
item.description: Policymakers, mental health experts and those working on the front line will use the National Suicide Prevention Outcomes Map to gauge what services are working to prevent people from reaching critical crisis points.

item.title: Conservation groups 'concerned' by exotic pet increase call for greater regulation
item.description: Australian and international authorities are "struggling" to keep up with regulating and policing the exotic pet market as demand surges.

item.title: 'Confusopoly' blamed as power bill messages befuddle consumers
item.description: Some electricity users have been hit with higher charges, despite all the talk of the lower default market offers. Retailers say a rebalancing of supply and usage charges is the cause, but the regulator is investigating.

item.title: Schools close, railways urge against travel as Europe heatwave intensifies
item.description: Schools are being ordered to close in the UK and railways are urging against non-essential travel across the continent as record-breaking heat takes hold.

item.title: Tasmania Devils VFL captain Jye Menzie on growing up in Bridgewater
item.description: Jye Menzie grew up in the north of Hobart, known for some of the nation's worst socio-economic outcomes and a high level of disadvantage. But the Tasmania Devils VFL captain is proud of where he comes from, and would not have it any other way.

item.title: Greyhound Racing SA hands four-year ban to 'remorseful' trainer Jack Clayton Trengove
item.description: One of SA greyhound racing's most prominent identities has been disqualified after a 12-month inquiry by the sport's controlling body.

item.title: US Senate calls for Trump to end Iran war in bipartisan war resolutions vote
item.description: It is the 10th time the Senate has tried to stop the war, and the outcome, on a vote of 50–48, is a stunning turnaround from past efforts.

item.title: World Cup: Cristiano Ronaldo roars back to life, with his moments off the ball the most telling
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo came into Portugal’s second World Cup group game under a firestorm of criticism. He answered with two goals, and important signs he is willing to be a team player.

item.title: NSW budget offers short-term sugar hit but there could be bitterness to come
item.description: Faced with rising interest rates, high grocery prices and bloated bills, the cost-of-living help announced in Tuesday's budget will only make a minor dent in household budgets, Nick Dole writes.

item.title: Why was Lake Cargelligo triple murderer Julian Ingram granted police bail?
item.description: Sophie Quinn sought police protection from her former partner. Less than two months later he murdered her and two others during a shooting spree at Lake Cargelligo.

item.title: Half a billion litres of improperly treated sewage released into Tasmanian waterways in a year
item.description: More than half a billion litres of improperly treated sewage were released into the state's waterways in the 2024 to 2025 financial year, including at popular swimming spots such as Launceston's Cataract Gorge.

item.title: Gilmour Space builds defence capability with first hypersonic rocket test in North Queensland
item.description: Hypersonic rockets will be used to test Defence and science equipment in extreme conditions.

item.title: Gilmour Space Technologies' rocket launch ended shortly after lift-off
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Australia's Emerson Jones and Storm Hunter advance in Wimbledon qualifying
item.description: Emerson Jones and Storm Hunter advance to the second round of Wimbledon's qualifying stage, but some of their fellow Australians are not as fortunate.

item.title: Grandfather recounts aftermath of fatal Pakistani police shooting of Perth girl Hania Ahmed
item.description: The grandfather of Australian schoolgirl Hania Ahmed recounts the aftermath of her fatal shooting, as a senior Pakistani lawyer takes aim at the "uncontrolled" police department at the centre of her death.

item.title: Pauline Hanson says Socceroos represent her vision of monoculture — as it happened
item.description: Hanson says she is "delighted" monoculturalism is being debated and says the Australian soccer team represents her vision for a national "monoculture". Take a look back at how the day unfolded.

item.title: Australian biosecurity investigates suspected illegal sales of imported peanuts
item.description: Australian biosecurity authorities are probing claims imported raw peanuts are being sold illegally without quarantine checks and exposing the nation's own peanut industry to potential plant diseases.

item.title: Funding in NSW budget for 'world-class' robotic surgery in Orange
item.description: A long-running campaign to secure robotic surgery in western NSW has succeeded with the NSW budget allocating almost $10 million for equipment.

item.title: Carley Scott wins long-running Federal Court case against space start-up Equatorial Launch Australia
item.description: Once a promising space start-up chief executive, Carley Scott was terminated from her job and left millions out of pocket. Now she's finally found vindication, with a court ruling in her favour.

item.title: Australia records 113-run win over Pakistan to remain unbeaten at Women's T20 World Cup
item.description: Australia cruises to a comfortable 113-run victory over Pakistan at Headingley, but there may be concern over a finger injury to one of their stars.

item.title: Latest inflation data increases rate-hike risk, ASX rises — as it happened
item.description: The chances of an interest rate increase at the next RBA meeting have risen after the release of mixed inflation data by the ABS. The Australian share market finished the day higher despite a major sell-off across US tech. Look back on our live blog.

item.title: Six men charged over alleged insider fraud plot against construction giant Shadforth
item.description: Two Queensland men are sentenced after pleading guilty to defrauding a major Queensland construction company in an insider plot.

item.title: Andy Burnham describes his first day back in parliament
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Siri Hustvedt reflects on the death of her husband, Paul Auster, in her new memoir, Ghost Stories
item.description: The author of What I Loved has published a moving memoir drawing on letters and journals documenting the last days of Paul Auster, her partner in life and writing.

item.title: Pilbara's North Pole Dome confirmed again to be world's oldest known asteroid crater
item.description: Researchers find a crater in WA's East Pilbara is 3 billion years old, but it has sparked debate in the scientific community.

item.title: Chemical exposure limits put on hold despite Safe Work Australia advice
item.description: A majority of state and territory governments have put stricter workplace exposure limits on hold for nine hazardous chemicals.

item.title: Biosecurity Tasmania on alert over first detection of invasive serpentine leafminer
item.description: The insect has been found at a commercial grower in Tasmania's north-west. It damages the leaves of the plants it attacks and can reduce crop yield.

item.title: World Cup: England's hunt for positivity more fruitful than an attacking spark against resolute Ghana defence
item.description: England say they need to stay positive after a dour goal-less draw against Ghana, positivity that is hopefully easier to find than shots on target were against the Black Stars' low block in Boston.

item.title: Kangaroos dominate Australia's AFLW squad for Ireland international
item.description: North Melbourne has the most players of any club selected in Australia's squad for the inaugural AFLW representative match against Ireland.

item.title: Fossil find plugs huge gap in Australia's marsupial record
item.description: A few teeth found in an outback fossil deposit have helped fill in a 30-million-year blind spot in Australia's evolutionary history.

item.title: Karl Stefanovic's interview with Tommy Robinson pulled from social media and podcast platforms
item.description: Today show host-turned-podcaster Karl Stefanovic has had his interview with far-right extremist Tommy Robinson taken down from social media and podcast platforms.

item.title: Limestone Coast farming couple Brian and Val Collins celebrate 70 years of love on the land
item.description: Farming couple Bill and Val Collins share their secrets to a long marriage as they celebrate their platinum wedding anniversary.

item.title: World Cup: Why Socceroos won't be playing for a draw against Paraguay
item.description: A draw would be enough for the Socceroos to progress from their World Cup group, but according to defender Jason Geria, playing for anything less than a win does not sit right with this group.

item.title: Bondi Beach terror attack hero Ahmed Al Ahmed pleads not guilty to alleged assault on father
item.description: Ahmed Al Ahmed has pleaded not guilty to allegations he assaulted and intimidated his father during an incident in March.

item.title: Headline inflation eases, but underlying inflation highest since 2024
item.description: Underlying inflation allows economists to look beneath the daily noise of random price movements to see what the economy's broader inflationary pressures are looking like.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Cristiano Ronaldo's records, hydration break update, Donald Trump's special job
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo makes a bit of World Cup history as Gianni Infantino offers an update on the future of the hydration breaks and Donald Trump is given role at this year's final. Here are today's quick hits.

item.title: Daryl Braithwaite's heartbroken by fans' appreciation after announcing end to live performances
item.description: The ARIA Hall of Fame inductee speaks emotionally to the ABC about his decision to give up live performances, saying it has broken his heart to see how appreciative people have been.

item.title: Papua New Guinea reverses ban on Australian poultry imports due to bird flu
item.description: Australia's largest chicken meat buyer, Papua New Guinea, has reversed its decision to suspend Australian poultry and egg imports due to the H5N1 bird flu outbreak.

item.title: Ebola virus outbreak in Democratic Republic of Congo one of worst recorded
item.description: There are now more than 1,000 confirmed cases of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo and 267 people have died, local officials say.

item.title: Squash star Greg Lobban's move from Scotland to represent Australia a challenge to identity
item.description: Scotland's number one squash player Greg Lobban has conformed he will switch to Australia ahead of the 2028 Olympic Games, but acknowledged the decision was not easy.

item.title: Jessie James Tumaliuan sentenced to 26 years' jail for estranged wife's murder
item.description: Jessie James Tumaliuan has been sentenced to more than two decades in jail for the violent murder of his estranged wife in March last year.

item.title: Pauline Hanson says her vision of 'monoculture' is like the Socceroos
item.description: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson has likened her vision of a "monocultural" Australia to the Socceroos as Opposition Leader Angus Taylor clarifies that he would still seek "a version" of multiculturalism.

item.title: Listen to David Dowsett on ABC Wide Bay to win double passes to Brolga Theatre show
item.description: Tune in to David Dowsett for ABC Wide Bay's breakfast show and your chance to win a double pass to a show of your choice at the Brolga Theatre.

item.title: Baby Kobi's death at Whispering Wall 'preventable' as coroner highlights 'errors'
item.description: A coroner finds the "upsetting" death of a nine-month-old baby in the Barossa Valley in a public murder-suicide by her "manipulative, self-absorbed and dangerous" father could have been prevented.

item.title: Police to review interactions with Virginia Giuffre prior to her death in Western Australia
item.description: The interactions Jeffery Epstein accuser Virginia Giuffre had with Australian police before her death will be reviewed, WA's police commissioner confirms.

item.title: Two women charged after allegedly trafficking cocaine, meth to Tasmania on Spirit ferry
item.description: Two women are alleged to have had traffickable quantities of meth and cocaine in their car when they evaded police upon exiting the Spirit of Tasmania ferry in Devonport.

item.title: FIFA World Cup: Socceroos should play Mat Ryan against Paraguay, Mark Schwarzer says
item.description: As the Socceroos prepare to face Paraguay in their final FIFA World Cup group game, Aussie great Mark Schwarzer says now is the time to bring captain Mat Ryan back into the team.

item.title: When are the Socceroos playing Paraguay? What time is Australia's World Cup game?
item.description: The Socceroos have one more match in the group stage as they look to make it to the knockout stages of the World Cup. Here's everything you need to know.

item.title: Housing Minister denies tax changes are major driver for market 'correction'
item.description: Clare O'Neil has acknowledged that the property market is undergoing a correction, but says the government's proposed tax changes are not the sole cause.

item.title: Here's what Nicola Sturgeon's husband Peter Murrell bought with embezzled SNP funds
item.description: Court documents have revealed what former Scottish first minister Nicola Sturgeon's husband Peter Murrell bought with funds he embezzled from the party over 12 years.

item.title: You may have heard of looksmaxxing but what about mummymaxxing?
item.description: Also known as mommymaxxing and mommaxxing — depending on which internet rabbit hole you're going down — mummymaxxing focuses on optimising motherhood and experts warn it could be harmful.

item.title: Canberra is known for its local shops, but while some are thriving others have fallen into disrepair
item.description: Local shops are a distinctive feature of Canberra, but while some are thriving, others have fallen into disrepair and offer no sense of community for nearby residents.

item.title: Christian Brothers says it is paying $1.7m a week to abuse victims and running out of funds
item.description: The Congregation of Christian Brothers Oceania Province has revealed it was seeking to sell off its 36 properties to pay abuse victims, who would be treated as creditors.

item.title: Indonesian company Ithaca Resources secures $200 million deal for Vitrinite assets
item.description: An Indonesian mining company has agreed to purchase a bankrupt Queensland company's assets, which include the Vulcan coal mine in the Bowen Basin.

item.title: Third Australian H5 bird flu case confirmed in SA, another suspected WA case
item.description: A third confirmed case of the deadly H5 bird flu has been found in a migratory bird on Australia's southern coast.

item.title: Evacuation plan to move ships through Strait of Hormuz being enacted
item.description: An evacuation plan to enable hundreds of ships to sail through the Strait of Hormuz is being enacted, according to the UN's International Maritime Organization.

item.title: Murray-Darling Basin communities have their say on future of the Basin Plan during review
item.description: Communities across the Murray-Darling Basin are calling for a better balance, as irrigators say water buybacks are hurting their communities.

item.title: US Congress passes resolution to halt military action in Iran, but don't expect war to end
item.description: The US Senate approved a war powers resolution seeking to block military action against Iran on Tuesday. But don't expect the war to end.

item.title: As Liberals and Nationals jump ship, has One Nation won the 'family feud' of Australian conservatives?
item.description: There's a "family feud" in Australia's political right, and right now it appears One Nation is winning.

item.title: The inside story of how one of Canberra's longest running cold cases, the Irma Palasics killing, was solved
item.description: Twenty-four years after the killing of a Canberra grandmother, a coffee cup discarded on an east coast road trip leads to the arrest and convictions of her killers.

item.title: South-east Queensland water bills to rise despite bulk freeze in state budget
item.description: South-east Queensland households will face a higher price hike on their water and sewerage bills next year.

item.title: New registry established as MND becomes 'notifiable disease' in NSW
item.description: An MND expert says the registry is a first step to help identify what causes the incurable disease and how it could be stopped.

item.title: Award-winning artist Jane Allan accused of copying second artwork
item.description: Australian artist Jane Allan, revealed to have previously copied another painter's work, is being accused of a second case of imitation.

item.title: Footage captures armed offenders at Townsville home, businesses
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Police believe Townsville gym shooting and suspicious shop fire are linked
item.description: Queensland police have released video of a gym shooting and a suspicious shop fire in Townsville in Feburary.

item.title: Gambling billionaire Laurence Escalante bail varied to visit mother in Sydney
item.description: A billionaire tech investor accused of assaulting his former partner and stealing expensive gifts he had bought her has his bail varied so he can visit his mother in Sydney.

item.title: Court told alleged cult leader 'Little Pebble' groomed child to be his queen
item.description: A complainant has told a court that alleged NSW South Coast cult leader William "Little Pebble" Kamm groomed her for more than a decade.

item.title: NT Labor politician Manuel Brown caught speeding multiple times, years after fatal crash conviction
item.description: Manuel Brown had his licence suspended for three months last October, which was not disclosed publicly at the time. The Arafura MLA also failed to disclose a conviction over a fatal crash when he was initially preselected for Labor in 2023.

item.title: Wayne Bennett says South Sydney Rabbitohs' NRL injury issues just bad luck
item.description: Not for the first time, South Sydney is counting the cost of an outsized injury toll, with questions being asked about their training surface.

item.title: Rinehart complex for homeless veterans named after Ben Roberts-Smith
item.description: Australia's richest person has opened a facility for homeless veterans in a beachside Perth suburb.

item.title: Neil the seal is back bigger than ever and that's a problem for his wellbeing
item.description: Neil, an elephant seal well known to Tasmanians, has returned to land and a new video shows him being coerced off a road for his own safety.

item.title: Wednesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Victorian Liberal MP claims assault by party colleague
item.description: The Victorian Liberal Party is in chaos after an MP claimed they were assaulted by a member of their own party room.

item.title: ASIO chief Mike Burgess defends resourcing decisions and warns of 'unprecedented number of threats'
item.description: Australia's domestic spy chief, Mike Burgess, has defended the agency's resourcing decisions in the years leading up to the Bondi terror attack, arguing his agency is grappling with an "unprecedented" volume of threats.

item.title: Karl Stefanovic expected to leave Channel Nine after Tommy Robinson podcast
item.description: Today show host Karl Stefanovic is set to leave Channel Nine after a podcast interview with British far-right figure Tommy Robinson, Nine Newspapers is reporting.

item.title: France detects first Ebola case in doctor returning from DRC
item.description: France has confirmed its first case of Ebola, a doctor who had flown back from the Democratic Republic of Congo.

item.title: Sydney, Melbourne tipped to lead housing price drops as market cools
item.description: House price falls of up to 7 and 8 per cent in Sydney and Melbourne have been forecast for the year ahead, according to Domain. But it's far from doom and gloom for existing owners, with resale profits hitting a record.

item.title: Live: Haiti stuns Morocco early as Scotland looks to make World Cup history
item.description: Switzerland claims its World Cup group with a tense victory over Canada as Scotland prepares to take on Brazil to secure a knockout spot. Follow live.

item.title: Anna saw gambling as harmless fun. Here's what she wished she knew
item.description: Experts say the gambling industry is increasingly targeting women, and there are potential risks with a novelty bet.

item.title: How a night in the Swedish forest healed my inner 14-year-old
item.description: I've hated camping since I was a teenager, but embracing the spirit of Sweden's Allemansrätten — the right to camp wherever you like — changed everything.

item.title: Can you guess these Australian movies from a single still?
item.description: Think you know your Mad Max from your Muriel's Wedding? Take our Australian film quiz to find out.

item.title: Hanson's 'monoculture' own goal should have been a free kick for Taylor
item.description: Pauline Hanson has chosen one of Australia's most popular national sporting brands and visible expressions of cultural diversity in her pitch for a "monoculture".

item.title: As Europe struggles with soaring temperatures, Australia's heatwave history could teach valuable lessons
item.description: Europe's record-breaking heatwave has highlighted its vulnerability to climate change. But countries could look to Australia's experience with scorching summers to learn valuable lessons.

item.title: Nitrous oxide imports surge as Australian doctors report increasing harm
item.description: The importation of nitrous oxide has exploded in the last decade. Nangs are increasingly being used by some to chase a high and causing significant damage.

item.title: Vanuatu demands France hand back remote Matthew and Hunter Islands in the Pacific
item.description: Tensions are rising between Vanuatu and France as they negotiate a sovereignty dispute over two volcanic islands, believed to be inhabited by a god.

item.title: Worker accommodation opens in Horsham but more housing needed
item.description: The first stage of a $9 million private worker accommodation development, designed to make it easier to attract essential workers to western Victoria, has reached completion.

item.title: Royal Society of Tasmania turning colonial maritime art album into a digital flip book
item.description: The Royal Society of Tasmania has owned an album of Owen Stanley's maritime artworks since 1900, but its delicate nature meant it could not be put on display. Now, the society is converting the album into a digital flip book so everyone can see it.

item.title: Dutch fans take over US town amid World Cup frenzy
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Bosnian fans lined up outside World Cup match
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: A bassy Viking row and a living statue: The best World Cup fan traditions
item.description: Every four years, the World Cup provides the perfect backdrop for fans to showcase their culture's traditional football rituals in front of millions.

item.title: Australian fashion designers faced with 'roadblocks' fighting fast-fashion duplicates
item.description: Small Australian fashion designers who have had their designs copied by overseas ultra-fast fashion companies say they have hit continuous roadblocks trying to get support.

item.title: Newcastle Knights player Asu Kepaoa banned for six weeks after using homophobic slur
item.description: Newcastle Knights player Asu Kepaoa is handed a six-week ban for using a homophobic slur during a NSW Cup match.

item.title: Showers to chase away the chill of Queensland's winter
item.description: A warm change will arrive in Queensland this weekend, along with scattered rain and warnings of strong winds in the north.

item.title: Stella Magnisalis fights to have ex-policeman husband kept behind bars
item.description: A domestic violence survivor speaks out about her fight to keep her former detective ex-husband behind bars as he attempts to be released on parole.

item.title: Former Liberal vice-president Teena McQueen dies after short illness
item.description: Teena McQueen, who served as the federal Liberal Party vice-president from 2017 to 2023, died on Wednesday after a battle with a short but severe medical condition.

item.title: Sydney MP pushes to bring human composting to Australia as burial costs rise
item.description: Advocates say human composting offers a sustainable, greener alternative to burials and crematoriums, and can also provide a cheaper and equitable option after death.

item.title: Family of teen killed in dog attack pushes for changes to ownership laws
item.description: Annalyse Blyton was killed in a dog attack in the NSW Hunter Valley last year. Her family is pushing for changes to dog ownership laws.

item.title: Puppies stolen from Brisbane animal shelter
item.description: Police say two Staffordshire bull terriers were taken from the refuge just before midnight.

item.title: Road train fitted with CT scanner screens remote Northern Territory residents for lung cancer
item.description: A road train fitted with a battery-powered CT scanner is travelling across the NT outback to screen remote community residents for lung cancer, in the jurisdiction with Australia's highest diagnosis and mortality rates for the disease.

item.title: How behaviour and biology decide the outbreak equation
item.description: The bugs that cause Ebola, hantavirus and diphtheria are microscopic, but many of the barriers to controlling them are human-sized.

item.title: Socceroos lose Jacob Italiano to injury ahead of World Cup clash with Paraguay
item.description: Wing-back Jacob Italiano is ruled out of Australia's crucial World Cup clash with Paraguay after suffering a groin injury.

item.title: Developer gets green-light to turn Sydney boarding house into luxury homes after tenants rehoused
item.description: A rundown boarding house which was once a haven for vulnerable people in inner Sydney is set to be converted into luxury apartments.

item.title: Live: Teal MPs launch new Community Strong Australia party
item.description: Two Sydney teal independent MPs have launched a new centrist political party, with ambitions of picking up seats in the Senate. Follow live.

item.title: Military-grade thermal imaging and helicopters were deployed to achieve the complete eradication of feral pigs from Australia's third-largest island.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Kangaroo Island declared feral pig-free two years after last sightings
item.description: Devastating bushfires provide Kangaroo Island with a unique silver lining as it becomes the largest island in the world to eradicate feral pigs.

item.title: Live: ASX set to edge higher ahead of jobs data, and oil slides
item.description: The ASX is set for another small gain after a mixed Wall St session and another retreat in oil prices. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

item.title: What the Socceroos need to progress to knockout stage of 2026 FIFA World Cup
item.description: The Socceroos will finish either second of third in their World Cup group. Second means qualification for the knockout rounds. Third is a bit more complicated.

item.title: Comiskey Group appeals $15 million Sunshine Coast Council bill in court
item.description: The developers behind the controversial Coochin Fields festival site are fighting a $15 million council bill in court, with the state government saying the charges are "outrageous".

item.title: 'ISIS bride' on temporary exclusion order issued permit to return home
item.description: The woman, who is an Australian citizen, had been issued a temporary exclusion order and was living in a refugee camp in Syria.

item.title: SA wildlife carers did not expect to be 'ground zero' for Australia's third H5 bird flu case
item.description: A South Australian wildlife rescue group is working with local authorities after Australia's third confirmed case of H5 bird flu was detected in a migratory bird.
